preview_text:
The model favors Brazil at 64% to win, with Scotland assigned an 12% chance of victory. The draw outcome carries nearly a quarter probability, suggesting Scotland has modest hope of earning a result rather than outright upset.

The win-loss spread is wide. Brazil's 52-point edge over Scotland in win probability reflects a significant quality gap in the model's assessment. However, the substantial draw probability—higher than Scotland's win chance—indicates the match carries genuine competitive structure. A stalemate remains plausible, tempering any sense of inevitable Brazilian dominance.

The model and market are closely aligned. The sportsbook consensus places Brazil at 65%, just 1.6 percentage points above the model, while the draw sits 4 points lower in market pricing. Polymarket prices align similarly with the model. These minor divergences fall within typical consensus variance and suggest broad agreement on match structure.
